name: Sydney
summary: Sydney is a small harbour city on Cape Breton Island in eastern Canada. It is famous for the world's biggest fiddle on its waterfront and the wild moose, eagles and whales of nearby Cape Breton Highlands.
funFacts: The Big Fiddle on Sydney's waterfront is about 18 metres tall — that's the world's biggest fiddle, and it greets every cruise ship that sails into port.
The Cabot Trail loops 298 kilometres around Cape Breton's mountains and cliffs and is often called one of the most beautiful drives on Earth.
Wild moose, the tallest deer in the world, wander the highland forests and sometimes step right onto the road in front of cars.
Many Cape Bretoners still speak Scottish Gaelic, and you can hear fiddles and bagpipes playing at family parties called ceilidhs.
The nearby Fortress of Louisbourg is North America's biggest history rebuild — a whole 1740s French town with cannons, soldiers and bakers.
thingsToDo: Pose for a silly photo under the giant Big Fiddle right beside the cruise ship pavilion on Sydney's boardwalk.
Drive part of the Cabot Trail through Cape Breton Highlands National Park to look for moose and bald eagles.
Walk the wooden Skyline Trail boardwalk for huge ocean cliff views and a chance to spot whales far below.
Step inside the Fortress of Louisbourg, where soldiers in 1740s costume march, fire cannons and bake old-fashioned bread.
Visit Membertou Heritage Park to hear Mi'kmaq stories, see a birch-bark canoe and try the bone-and-stick game called Waltes.
Go underground at the Cape Breton Miners Museum in Glace Bay, led by retired coal miners who tell their own stories.

wildlife.name: Moose
wildlife.description: Huge brown deer with wide flat antlers that munches leaves in Cape Breton's highland forests.

wildlife.name: Bald Eagle
wildlife.description: Big brown bird of prey with a snowy-white head that soars over the coast hunting fish.

wildlife.name: Long-finned Pilot Whale
wildlife.description: Glossy black whale that swims in big family groups in the seas around Cape Breton.

wildlife.name: Snowshoe Hare
wildlife.description: Brown forest hare with giant furry feet that turn snowy white in winter for camouflage.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Mi'kmaw, the language of Cape Breton's First Nations people.

images.alt: A giant blue and brown fiddle sculpture stands tall on the Sydney waterfront beside the cruise pavilion.
The winding Cabot Trail hugs steep green cliffs above the sparkling blue ocean.
A wooden boardwalk leads down a green headland toward the wide ocean on the famous Skyline Trail.
Stone walls and tall steeples of the historic French Fortress of Louisbourg under a bright blue sky.
The brick Membertou Convention Centre and heritage park building near Sydney.
The Cape Breton Miners Museum building in Glace Bay near Sydney.
A male moose with wide antlers stands in a meadow in Cape Breton Highlands National Park.
A bald eagle with a white head and yellow beak perches in a tree.
images.credit: Steve Hillebrand / U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

images.alt: A group of glossy black long-finned pilot whales surfacing in the open ocean.
images.credit: Bill Thompson / U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

images.alt: A brown snowshoe hare sits among grass and wildflowers showing its big back feet.
